ruleset.name: Best Practices
ruleset.description: Rules which enforce generally accepted best practices.

rule.name: AvoidMessageDigestField
rule.message: MessageDigest fields are not thread-safe; declare a local instance instead.
rule.description:
`java.security.MessageDigest` is not thread-safe. Declaring it as a class field exposes it to concurrent access without explicit synchronisation, producing incorrect digests. Create a new instance per method call, or protect shared access with a mutex.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidMessageDigestField

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidPrintStackTrace
rule.message: Avoid printStackTrace(); use kotlin-logging or a SLF4J logger instead.
rule.description:
Avoid calling `Throwable.printStackTrace()`; use a proper logger instead. Stack traces printed to standard output can be lost in production and carry no log level, making them hard to control or suppress.
Kotlin idiomatic alternative: use kotlin-logging (`io.github.microutils:kotlin-logging`): - `private val log = KotlinLogging.logger {}` - `log.error(e) { "operation failed" }`
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidPrintStackTrace

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidStringBufferField
rule.message: StringBuffer/StringBuilder fields can grow large; prefer buildString'{'...'}'or a local instance.
rule.description:
`StringBuffer` and `StringBuilder` instances can grow considerably and, if held as class fields across long-lived objects, may cause memory leaks.
Kotlin idiomatic alternatives: - Use `buildString { append(...) }` for one-shot construction (returns a `String` directly). - Create a local `StringBuilder` inside the function that needs it. - Prefer string templates (`"$var"`) which the Kotlin compiler optimises automatically.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/AvoidStringBufferField

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: FunctionNameTooShort
rule.message: Function names should have non-cryptic and clear names.
rule.description: Function names should be easy to understand and describe the intention. Makes developers happy. Short names that are conventional in Kotlin or platform idioms (e.g. event listener `on`, Android density-pixel helper `dp`) can be excluded via the `allowedNames` property.

property.name: allowedNames
property.description: Short function names that are conventional and should not be flagged.

rule.example:
fun cl() {} // violation
fun calculateLayout() {} // no violation
fun on(listener: () -> Unit) {} // no violation -- 'on' is in allowedNames by default
override fun on(event: String) {} // no violation -- overrides are always excluded
fun dp(value: Int): Int = (value * 2.5f).toInt() // no violation -- 'dp' is in allowedNames by default (Android convention)

rule.name: LooseCoupling
rule.message: Use the interface type (List/Set/Map) instead of a concrete implementation type as the declared type.
rule.description:
Declaring fields or properties with a concrete collection type (`ArrayList`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, etc.) tightly couples callers to an implementation detail. Declare the type as the appropriate interface (`List`, `Set`, `Map`, `MutableList`, `MutableSet`, `MutableMap`) so the implementation can be swapped without breaking API consumers.
In Kotlin, prefer the idiomatic factory functions: - `listOf()` / `mutableListOf()` -> returns `MutableList` - `setOf()` / `mutableSetOf()` -> returns `MutableSet` - `mapOf()` / `mutableMapOf()` -> returns `MutableMap`
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/LooseCoupling

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //PropertyDeclaration[ not(ancestor::FunctionBody) and ( pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.ArrayList') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.LinkedList') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.HashSet') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.LinkedHashSet') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.TreeSet') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.HashMap') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.LinkedHashMap') or pmd-kotlin:typeIsExactly('java.util.TreeMap') ) ]

rule.name: PreserveStackTrace
rule.message: The original exception should be passed as the cause when throwing a new exception from a catch block.
rule.description: When catching an exception and throwing a new one from within a catch block, the original exception should be passed as the `cause` argument so that the original stack trace is preserved. Without it, the root cause of the problem is lost. Exception: catch variables named with the `ignored` prefix or underscore are intentionally discarded and are not flagged.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: SystemPrintln
rule.message: Usage of System.out/err; use kotlin-logging or another logging framework.
rule.description:
Calls to `System.out.print*`, `System.err.print*`, or `System.out.printf` are typically left-over debug statements. Use a structured logging framework so output can be controlled by log level and configuration without redeploying.
Kotlin idiomatic alternative: kotlin-logging (`io.github.microutils:kotlin-logging`): - `private val log = KotlinLogging.logger {}` - `log.info { "message" }`
Note: Kotlin's built-in top-level `println()` also maps to `System.out` and should be replaced in production code. This rule detects the explicit `System.out/err` variants; treat `println()` / `print()` calls as violations in code review.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/bestpractices.xml/SystemPrintln

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: UseCollectionIsEmpty
rule.message: Use isEmpty() or isNotEmpty() instead of comparing size to 0
rule.description: Prefer `isEmpty()` or `isNotEmpty()` over comparing `size` (or `count()`) to zero. Using the dedicated methods is more idiomatic Kotlin and more readable.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: UseStandardCharsets
rule.message: Use a StandardCharsets constant instead of Charset.forName(String)
rule.description: Use the constants from `java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets` instead of calling `Charset.forName(String)`. The constants avoid the need to handle `UnsupportedEncodingException` and provide a small performance advantage because no lookup through the charset cache is needed.

rule.name: LocalVariableShadowsParameter
rule.message: Local variable ''{0}'' shadows a parameter of the enclosing function.
rule.description: A local variable declaration uses the same name as a parameter of the enclosing function. This shadows the parameter and may lead to confusion about which value is used.
